entry_thesis: Concerns: Pivotal study failed primary endpoint with p=0.97; Statistical uncertainties in post-hoc analyses; Serious neurologic adverse events from intrathecal administration; NfL not an established surrogate endpoint; Confirmatory studies delayed or insufficient; Positives: Strong target engagement shown by SOD1 reduction; Substantial NfL reduction correlating with disease progression; Post-hoc analyses suggest clinical benefit; Severe unmet need for rare SOD1-ALS; FDA regulatory flexibility for accelerated approval

Concerns: Primary endpoint not met in pivotal study (p=0.97); Reliance on post-hoc analyses after unblinding; Study underpowered due to slower than expected decline; Safety signals: myelitis, radiculitis, aseptic meningitis, papilledema, elevated intracranial pressure; Uncertainty whether NfL reduction is reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit; Positives: Large and consistent reduction in plasma NfL (55% vs. 12% increase in placebo); Clear target engagement shown by CSF SOD1 reduction (38% difference); Severe unmet need in rapidly fatal SOD1-ALS with no targeted therapies; Extremely rare disease makes additional trials challenging; OLE data suggestive of clinical benefit with longer treatment duration
